options.name: -ignore_ref_pin_shape
options.description: Specifies that the aligned pin will ignore matching of shape with the reference pin.

options.name: -keepLayer
options.description: Specifies whether to keep the current pin layer.

options.name: -legalizePin
options.description: Specifies that the aligned pin will be tried for nearby legalized location while placing the pin.

options.name: -markFixed
options.description: Specifies that the pins that are moved by this command will have a Fixed status.

options.name: -moveFixedPin
options.description: Specifies that the pins with a Fixed status can also be moved.

options.name: -noSnap
options.description: Disables pins from being snapped to the routing grid.

options.name: -pinNames
options.description: Specifies the pin(s) of partition to be aligned between the facing edges with the reference block. You can use wildcards (*?) with this parameter.

options.name: -ptnInst
options.description: Specifies the target partition inst or hinst name (including top) whose pins will be aligned to the pins of the specified (or inferred) instance (or partition).

options.name: -refObj
options.description: Specifies the reference object where the specified pins of the target block will be aligned to the pins of the reference object.

options.name: -refType
options.description: Refers to type of refObj to be considered with wildcard
